para_10_p746_0.text:
• Avoid bending or damaging any portion of the buckle or latch plate.
• Do not bleach or dye belt webbing; use only mild soap and water.
• When installing safety belt anchor bolts, start bolts by hand to prevent cross-threading.
• Do not attempt any repairs on retractor mechanisms or covers. Replace defective assemblies with new ones.
• Replace any belts that are cut or damaged in any way.
• Do not install anything in trim panel which will obstruct the movement of safety belt.

proc_10_p746_0.text:
Inspect
1. All safety belt anchor bolts to verify that they are secure.
2. Safety belt buckle, making sure it locks and unlocks easily.
3. After inserting latch into buckle, tug sharply on belt, checking that the buckle remains locked.
4. Fully extend shoulder belt to make sure there are no twists or tears in it.
5. Allow the shoulder belt to fully retract, making sure it does so easily.
steps: All safety belt anchor bolts to verify that they are secure.
Safety belt buckle, making sure it locks and unlocks easily.
After inserting latch into buckle, tug sharply on belt, checking that the buckle remains locked.
Fully extend shoulder belt to make sure there are no twists or tears in it.
Allow the shoulder belt to fully retract, making sure it does so easily.

proc_10_p746_1.text:
Running Test (E-ALR Safety Belts)
1. Fasten safety belt.
2. Drive vehicle at 16 km/h (10 mph) and apply brakes firmly.
3. Verify that the safety belt locks and does not allow your body to move forward.
4. If the belt does not lock during the running test, remove retractor and perform the static test.
steps: Fasten safety belt.
Drive vehicle at 16 km/h (10 mph) and apply brakes firmly.
Verify that the safety belt locks and does not allow your body to move forward.
If the belt does not lock during the running test, remove retractor and perform the static test.

caut_10_p746_0.text: CAUTION: Conduct this test only in a safe area. Do not conduct test on the open road. A large empty parking lot is much more suitable.

proc_10_p746_2.text:
Static Test
1. Remove retractor assembly.
2. Tilt the retractor slowly.
3. Verify that the belt can be pulled out of the retractor at an inclination of 15 degrees or less and that it cannot be pulled out at over 45 degrees of inclination.
steps: Remove retractor assembly.
Tilt the retractor slowly.
Verify that the belt can be pulled out of the retractor at an inclination of 15 degrees or less and that it cannot be pulled out at over 45 degrees of inclination.

Remove or Disconnect
Problem: Belt locks when door is opened.
  - Possible Cause: Battery over-discharged
  - Correction: Charge battery.
  - Possible Cause: Automatic seat belt control module fuse blown
  - Correction: Repair and replace.
  - Possible Cause: Control module power circuit or ground open
  - Correction: Repair.
  - Possible Cause: Door switch open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Door latch switch open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Safety belt retractor solenoid open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Diode open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Control module defective
  - Correction: Replace.

tbl_10_p748_1.text:
Remove or Disconnect
Problem: Safety belt cannot lock.
  - Possible Cause: Door switch and door latch switch shorted
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Safety belt retractor lock defective
  - Correction: Replace.
  - Possible Cause: Control module defective
  - Correction: Replace.

tbl_10_p748_2.text:
Remove or Disconnect
Problem: Safety belt reminder lamp turns ON even when door is closed and safety belt is buckled.
  - Possible Cause: If safety belt cannot be locked, refer to above item.
  - Possible Cause: If safety belt can be locked: Lock cancel sensor switch open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: If safety belt can be locked: Negative (–) side of lamp shorted to chassis
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: If safety belt can be locked: Warning lamp check switch shorted
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: If safety belt can be locked: Control module defective
  - Correction: Replace.

tbl_10_p748_3.text:
Remove or Disconnect
Problem: Door warning lamp stays ON or flashing even when door is closed.
  - Possible Cause: When ON: Negative (–) side of lamp shorted to chassis
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: When ON: Warning lamp check switch shorted
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: When flashing: Door switch shorted
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Whether ON or flashing, control module defective
  - Correction: Replace.

tbl_10_p748_4.text:

Problem: Safety belt reminder lamp fails to turn ON or buzzer fails to sound even when safety belt is unbuckled.
  - Possible Cause: Safety belt switch open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Fuse blown (15A or 20A fuse)
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Control module power circuit or ground open
  - Correction: Repair.
  - Possible Cause: Defective control module
  - Correction: Replace.

tbl_10_p748_5.text:

  - Possible Cause: Door switch open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.

  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Control module power circuit or ground open

  - Possible Cause: Defective control module
  - Correction: Replace.

Problem: Safety belt reminder lamp and/or door warning lamp bulb cannot be checked.

  - Possible Cause: Warning lamp bulb burnt out
  - Correction: Replace.
  - Possible Cause: Warning lamp check switch open
  - Correction: Repair or replace.
  - Possible Cause: Defective control module
  - Correction: Replace.

fig_10_p751_0.text:
Figure 8 Retractor Diagnosis
1: RETRACTOR
2: WEBBING
3: BATTERY
4: DOOR

proc_10_p751_0.text:
Inspect
1. Webbing to ensure that it is free from cuts or damage.
2. Safety belt and buckle for secure connection.
3. Pull webbing sharply out of retractor. Retractor should lock webbing and prevent further release until tension is relieved.
4. Connect a fused jumper from a battery positive (+) source to safety belt assembly connector cavity B. Connect another jumper from safety belt assembly connector cavity A to chassis ground. With jumpers connected and retractor solenoids energized, the retractor should not lock the safety belt webbing. If webbing locks, replace the safety belt assembly (Figure 8).
steps: Webbing to ensure that it is free from cuts or damage.
Safety belt and buckle for secure connection.
Pull webbing sharply out of retractor. Retractor should lock webbing and prevent further release until tension is relieved.

Connect a fused jumper from a battery positive (+) source to safety belt assembly connector cavity B. Connect another jumper from safety belt assembly connector cavity A to chassis ground. With jumpers connected and retractor solenoids energized, the retractor should not lock the safety belt webbing. If webbing locks, replace the safety belt assembly (Figure 8).

para_10_p751_0.text: Resistance between cavities C and D with retractor solenoid de-energized (Figure 8). If resistance is more than 0.3 ohms, replace safety belt assembly.

proc_10_p751_1.text:
Install or Connect
1. Safety belt assembly, retractor fixing bolt and nuts. Tighten: Retractor fixing bolts and nuts to 35 N·m (26 lb.ft.).
2. Retractor lead wire.
3. Lap belt anchor bolt. Tighten: Lap belt stud to 3.5 N·m (26 lb.ft.).
4. Shoulder bracket and then shoulder bolt stud. Tighten: Shoulder bolt stud to 5.5 N·m (49 lb.in.).
5. Door panel.
steps: Safety belt assembly, retractor fixing bolt and nuts. Tighten: Retractor fixing bolts and nuts to 35 N·m (26 lb.ft.).
Retractor lead wire.
Lap belt anchor bolt. Tighten: Lap belt stud to 3.5 N·m (26 lb.ft.).
Shoulder bracket and then shoulder bolt stud. Tighten: Shoulder bolt stud to 5.5 N·m (49 lb.in.).
Door panel.

impo_10_p751_0.text: IMPORTANT: The automatic safety belt control module is a solid state component. Do not measure resistance.

Voltage at each cavity of automatic safety belt control module connector. If observed voltage is not within allowable tolerance, make necessary electrical repairs (Figure 7).

maint_0b_summary.text:
Scheduled Maintenance — Maintenance Schedule
Schedule I, Item 1: Engine Oil and Oil Filter Change — every 3000 miles / 3 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule I, Item 2: Chassis Lubrication — every Every Other Oil Change.
Schedule I, Item 3: Tire and Wheel Inspection and Rotation — every 6000 miles (whichever occurs first).
Schedule I, Item 4: Water Pump Belt Inspection — every 30000 miles / 30 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule I, Item 5: Cooling System Service — every see notes. Emission control service item. Note: See Explanation of Scheduled Maintenance Services
Schedule I, Item 6: Brake Fluid Inspection — every 15000 miles / 15 months (whichever occurs first).
Schedule I, Item 7: Transaxle Service — every see notes. Note: See Explanation of Scheduled Maintenance Services
Schedule I, Item 8: Spark Plug Replacement — every 30000 miles (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule I, Item 9: Spark Plug Wires Replacement — every 60000 miles / 60 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule I, Item 10: Air Cleaner Element Replacement — every 30000 miles / 30 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule I, Item 11: Fuel Tank Cap and Lines Inspection — every 15000 miles / 15 months (whichever occurs first).
Schedule II, Item 1: Engine Oil and Oil Filter Change — every 7500 miles / 7.5 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule II, Item 2: Chassis Lubrication — every 7500 miles / 7.5 months (whichever occurs first).
Schedule II, Item 3: Tire and Wheel Inspection and Rotation — every 7500 miles. Note: Then every 15000 miles
Schedule II, Item 4: Water Pump Belt Inspection — every 30000 miles / 30 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule II, Item 5: Cooling System Service — every see notes. Emission control service item. Note: See Explanation of Scheduled Maintenance Services
Schedule II, Item 6: Brake Fluid Inspection — every 15000 miles / 15 months (whichever occurs first).
Schedule II, Item 7: Transaxle Service — every see notes. Note: See Explanation of Scheduled Maintenance Services
Schedule II, Item 8: Spark Plug Replacement — every 30000 miles (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule II, Item 9: Spark Plug Wires Replacement — every 60000 miles / 60 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule II, Item 10: Air Cleaner Element Replacement — every 30000 miles / 30 months (whichever occurs first). Emission control service item.
Schedule II, Item 11: Fuel Tank Cap and Lines Inspection — every 15000 miles / 15 months (whichever occurs first).
